title: Suryakumar Yadav's batting woes hit struggling Mumbai Indians
content: The Indian 'Clasico' between Chennai Super Kings and Mumbai Indians holds little playoff significance, but is crucial for individual players like Suryakumar Yadav. Facing a poor IPL season, Yadav's form is under scrutiny, with his ambition of leading India to the 2028 Olympics potentially at risk. Coach Mahela Jayawardene defended his player, highlighting his talent and past brilliance.

title: Final moments of mother and son found holding each other in MP boat tragedy
content: Search teams recovered the eighth and ninth bodies from Bargi Dam, including a mother, Marina Massey, and her four-year-old son, Trishaan, still embracing each other. Despite wearing life jackets, they were likely trapped in the lower cabin as the boat capsized. Officials believe Marina instinctively tried to protect her son in their final moments.

title: ‘Extraordinary iPhone demand’ pushes Apple to $111B quarter; Mac, iPad grow in India
content: Apple has surpassed expectations with a robust second quarter, reporting $111.2 billion in revenue, a 17% year-on-year surge.  Strong iPhone sales and consistent services growth fueled this success, with India emerging as a significant contributor.  CEO Tim Cook highlighted record-breaking iPhone and services performance, underscoring a powerful product lineup.

title: Jamieson’s fiery send-off to Sooryavanshi sparks outrage, debate erupts
content: A heated moment between Kyle Jamieson and young Vaibhav Sooryavanshi marked an IPL 2026 match. Jamieson's aggressive send-off after dismissing the 15-year-old drew reactions. Rajasthan Royals posted 225, led by Riyan Parag's 90. Delhi Capitals chased it down with KL Rahul and Pathum Nissanka starring. This win was Delhi's highest IPL chase.

title: Plane carrying pickleball players crashes in Texas, five killed
content: A small plane carrying five pickleball players from Amarillo crashed into trees near Wimberley late Thursday night, killing all on board. The Cessna 421C was en route to a tournament in New Braunfels when it disappeared from air traffic control scopes. Federal agencies are investigating the incident, with weather conditions noted as mostly cloudy prior to a reported thunderstorm.

title: Crew ‘abandoned’ cruise before it sank; no life jackets given, claims Bargi Dam survivor
content: A Bargi Dam cruise survivor alleges pilot and crew abandoned passengers before the boat sank, leaving them to fend for themselves. Despite a sudden storm, lifejackets were reportedly unavailable initially. Nine have died, six are missing, and a high-level probe has been ordered. Officials are struggling to confirm passenger numbers due to unregistered boardings.

title: CEO says ‘When Google CEO agrees with Elon, everyone must pay attention’; Musk responds
content: Elon Musk agreed with a post highlighting Google CEO Sundar Pichai's vision of space-based data centers becoming "the new normal" within a decade. This aligns with Musk's own long-held views on utilizing space for large-scale infrastructure. Pichai previously mentioned Google's "moon shot" to place data centers in orbit to harness solar energy, with a first step planned for 2027.

title: 'I don't need to answer critics': Riyan Parag's blunt take
content: Despite a recent fine for vaping, Riyan Parag shone with a brilliant 90, powering Rajasthan Royals to 225/6. However, Delhi Capitals chased down the target convincingly, securing a seven-wicket victory. Parag emphasised team success over individual performance, stating his primary goal is to secure wins for his team.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ mints over Rs 1785 cr as ‘Raja Shivaji’ takes lead on Friday
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' continues its steady box office run, grossing over Rs 1785 crore globally after 44 days. Despite facing new competition, including Riteish Deshmukh's 'Raja Shivaji' which took the lead on Friday, 'Dhurandhar 2' added Rs 1.05 crore on its 7th Friday, bringing its India net collection to Rs 1,136.14 crore.

title: 'Do not use Anthropic AI models': Goldman Sachs tells employees in Hong Kong
content: Goldman Sachs has prohibited its Hong Kong bankers from using Anthropic's Claude AI models, citing contract interpretations amid US-China tech tensions. This move, impacting coding and financial modeling, raises concerns for Hong Kong's financial hub status and other institutions using the AI. The restriction highlights broader worries about AI intellectual property theft and cybersecurity risks.

title: At least 6 killed after car crashes into stationary lorry in Telangana
content: Six individuals tragically lost their lives in Rajanna Sircilla, Telangana, after their speeding car collided with a stationary lorry on Saturday. The severe impact mangled the car, trapping the occupants. Authorities are investigating potential causes like poor visibility or driver fatigue, with a detailed probe underway.

title: ‘Blast them or make a deal’: Trump lays out choice for Iran, calls war criticism ‘treasonous’
content: US President Trump presented a stark choice on Iran: negotiate a settlement or face military escalation, though he personally prefers avoiding conflict. He expressed dissatisfaction with Iran's deal proposals, citing internal leadership disarray. Trump also labelled domestic criticism of the war effort as 'treasonous' and defended past US actions against Iran's nuclear program, vowing not to withdraw prematurely.

title: How job loss, debt pushed Indian youth to suicide in US
content: A 26-year-old Indian student in Chicago, Chandu Iraganaboyina, tragically took his own life due to joblessness and severe financial stress. Friends and community members are now working to repatriate his body to India, highlighting the immense pressure faced by some international students. Efforts are underway to understand how he obtained a firearm.

title: Mike Vrabel split response vs Cardi B-Offset breakup styles clash
content: Grammy winning rapper Cardi B and Jen Vrabel, wife of New England Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel, show different ways of handling cheating. Cardi B forgave her ex-husband but warned of consequences. She is no longer with him. She filed for a divorce in July 2024 after seven years of marriage. As for Jen Vrabel, she was seen with her husband Mike Vrabel after reports of infidelity with Dianna Russini did internet rounds. Their actions suggest personal choices in their respective relationships.

title: NFL Trade Rumor: Cowboys, Packers, Browns eye Alvin Kamara
content: Reports suggest New Orleans Saints running back Alvin Kamara and Arizona Cardinals RBs James Conner and Trey Benson need new homes. ESPN’s Mike Clay identified the Washington Commanders, Indianapolis Colts, Dallas Cowboys, Cincinnati Bengals, Green Bay Packers, Kansas City Chiefs, and Cleveland Browns as potential fits. Kamara’s veteran dual-threat ability makes him an interesting option for teams like Dallas Cowboys and Green Bay Packers, despite retirement concerns.

title: Maria Sharapova net worth 2026: $200m fortune from tennis & investments
content: Maria Sharapova, a tennis icon, amassed an estimated $180 million net worth primarily through lucrative endorsement deals and her candy brand, Sugarpova. Her remarkable career included winning all four Grand Slams and reaching world No. 1. After retiring in 2020, she continues to thrive in business and investments, maintaining her global recognition.

title: Mumbai family deaths: FDA finds no additives in watermelon; experts probe poison angle
content: Initial food adulteration suspicions in the Dokadia family's tragic deaths have been largely dismissed by FDA reports, which found no artificial additives in collected samples. While bacterial contamination and other toxins remain possibilities, forensic tests are now underway to pinpoint the exact cause of the sudden demise of the four family members after a Saturday night feast.

title: ‘Failure to provide support’: US to withdraw 5k troops from Germany amid widening Iran war rift
content: The Pentagon is withdrawing approximately 5,000 US troops from Germany, a move signaling heightened tensions with European allies over the Iran conflict. This decision follows sharp criticism from German Chancellor Friedrich Merz regarding Iran negotiations. The drawdown aims to return US troop levels in Europe to pre-Ukraine war figures, reflecting broader US frustration with NATO allies' perceived lack of support.

title: West Bengal polls: Lockdown in strongroom areas after TMC, BJP clash
content: Kolkata police have imposed strict prohibitory orders around EVM strongrooms following Trinamool's vote tampering claims and clashes with BJP workers. Security has been heightened with additional forces and sealed roads. The Election Commission vows to punish any disruption, emphasizing robust security at all counting centers statewide to ensure a peaceful electoral process.

title: ‘Love you darling’: Hardik Pandya’s reaction to MS Dhoni steals the show
content: MS Dhoni might return for IPL 2026. He was seen training with Chennai Super Kings. He met Mumbai Indians captain Hardik Pandya. Pandya sought Dhoni's advice on batting. Both CSK and MI are struggling this season. Dhoni's return could boost CSK's campaign. Rohit Sharma is also recovering from injury. Leadership changes are ongoing for both teams. Consistency remains a challenge.

title: ‘I want MS Dhoni’s five trophies’- KL Rahul after surpassing him on IPL list
content: KL Rahul downplayed his personal milestone of surpassing MS Dhoni on the IPL all-time run-scorers list, emphasizing team success and trophies as his ultimate goal. Despite individual achievements, Rahul stated his primary focus remains on winning the IPL, aiming to match Dhoni's five championship titles.

title: Amit Shah meets Ladakh leaders, including Sonam Wangchuk, ahead of talks
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah met with Leh leaders, including Sonam Wangchuk, focusing on spiritual matters during his visit. Shah assured that Ladakh-specific issues would be discussed in a sub-committee meeting on May 22, urging an open mind for dialogue. This meeting follows previous inconclusive talks and past protests demanding statehood and safeguards.

title: Will MS Dhoni finally play in IPL 2026? Hussey says ‘all of Chennai is waiting’
content: MS Dhoni's return to IPL 2026 is imminent as he makes steady progress from a calf injury, according to CSK batting coach Michael Hussey. Dhoni is focusing on regaining full running fitness, with the final decision on his availability resting with him. He has deliberately stayed away from the dugout to avoid distracting the team.

title: West Asia conflict a ‘war’, government firms can invoke ‘force majeure’ clause
content: The Finance Ministry has declared the West Asia conflict a "war," enabling government entities to invoke 'force majeure' for public contracts impacted by disruptions. This allows for penalty-free extensions of two to four months for affected companies, offering significant relief to sectors like defence and drone manufacturing facing supply chain issues.

title: Special Supreme Court bench to hear TMC plea on counting staff today
content: Trinamool Congress has moved the Supreme Court, challenging a directive mandating central government employees as counting supervisors or assistants for Bengal's assembly election vote count. The party argues this is arbitrary, discriminatory, and creates bias, especially with the BJP at the Centre. They claim it unfairly alters the counting table composition, undermining a level playing field and free elections.

title: Bangladesh MP flags possible refugee crisis from India
content: A Bangladeshi MP warns of a potential Muslim refugee influx from India, citing regional conspiracies possibly linked to recent elections in Assam and West Bengal. He drew parallels to Rohingya arrivals and cited remarks about expelling illegal Bangladeshis. Concerns were also raised about ongoing border killings and potential "push-in" situations, suggesting an emerging crisis.

title: 'Above normal' rainfall to ensure many parts of India aren't hotter than usual
content: While most of India may experience normal to below-normal temperatures this May due to increased rainfall, certain regions, including Himalayan foothills, eastern coast, and parts of Maharashtra and Gujarat, are predicted to face an above-normal number of heatwave days. Warmer nights are also anticipated across many areas, linked to climate change.

title: West Bengal elections: TMC alleges strongroom breaches, BJP mocks claim
content: Trinamool Congress has raised concerns with the Election Commission regarding alleged CCTV blackouts and unauthorized access to EVM strongrooms. BJP dismissed these claims as tactics by a party anticipating defeat. TMC representatives reported seeing individuals inside sealed rooms without party agents present, fueling suspicion ahead of counting day.

title: US-Iran war: Trump tells Congress hostilities ‘terminated’, sidesteps war powers deadline
content: US President Trump has declared hostilities with Iran have ended, despite ongoing US troop presence and persistent regional tensions. This assertion, made in a letter to Congress, aims to bypass the War Powers Resolution's requirement for military action authorization.

title: Middle East crisis: Air India to make food optional, help cut price of tickets
content: Air India is exploring unbundling meals and lounge access on domestic and short international flights to combat rising costs. Passengers could see ticket prices reduced by opting out of these services. This move aims to address the financial strain from increasing fuel prices and a depreciating rupee, while acknowledging India's price-sensitive market.

title: After Rahul Gandhi’s attack, government defends Great Nicobar project
content: The government has clarified the Rs 81,000 crore Great Nicobar Project, emphasizing its strategic importance for India's presence in the Andaman Sea and Southeast Asia. Officials stated that environmental safeguards and the protection of indigenous communities are core to the planning, with all statutory procedures and consultations meticulously followed to ensure no adverse impact on Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups.

title: KL Rahul, Nissanka power DC to record chase as Parag’s 90 goes in vain
content: KL Rahul and Pathum Nissanka's explosive opening stand powered Delhi Capitals to a stunning seven-wicket victory over Rajasthan Royals. Chasing a formidable 226, Delhi's top order laid a solid foundation, with Rahul and Nissanka scoring fifties. Despite Riyan Parag's brilliant 90, Rajasthan's total proved insufficient as Delhi completed a memorable chase.

title: Pawan Khera vs Himanta a political battle, says SC, gives relief to Congress leader
content: The Supreme Court granted anticipatory bail to Congress spokesperson Pawan Khera, deeming allegations against Assam CM's wife and her counter-allegations politically motivated. The court emphasized personal liberty, stating custodial interrogation wasn't warranted given the political overtones of the dispute.

title: Navy gets delivery of stealth frigate Mahendragiri; warship equipped with BrahMoS
content: India's naval strength is bolstered with the arrival of INS Mahendragiri, an advanced stealth frigate from Project 17A. This delivery by Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Ltd. highlights the nation's growing self-reliance in warship construction, featuring cutting-edge stealth, weaponry, and indigenous technology. The frigate represents a significant leap in naval design and capability.

title: UK: Hindu group bids against Christian-Muslim group for same piece of faith land
content: Northstowe's Hindu community faces a significant challenge as they bid for a faith/community land plot to establish their first temple.  Currently, families travel 40 miles for worship, impacting cultural connection.  Hindu Samaj Northstowe proposes an interfaith hub with prayer rooms and a temple, competing with a church-led bid offering multi-faith spaces.

title: From red to green to saffron? BJP eyes power shift in Bengal as Mamata defends her turf
content: West Bengal's political landscape, historically dominated by single parties for extended periods, is now witnessing an intense battle for the 2026 assembly elections. Chief minister Mamata Banerjee's 15-year rule faces a formidable challenge from the aggressively expanding BJP, which aims to end the TMC's reign. This election marks a critical juncture for the state's political legacy.

title: 'Never seen anything like it': Iran damaged 16 US military sites across 8 Gulf nations
content: Iran and its allies have reportedly damaged sixteen United States military sites across eight Middle Eastern nations. CNN reports, citing satellite images and official interviews, reveal significant degradation of radar, communications, and aircraft facilities. This conflict has already cost the US billions. Gulf allies expressed concerns about the alliance's resilience.

title: J&K: Omar Abdullah denies plan to drop Urdu in revenue jobs, slams PDP over ‘lies’
content: Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah refuted claims of removing Urdu from revenue jobs, stating a proposal is under consideration for public feedback, not a final decision. He challenged the PDP to produce an order, accusing them of misrepresenting the process.

title: Annu Kapoor backs ‘Dhurandhar’, says outsiders ruled industry
content: Veteran actor Annu Kapoor weighed in on the ongoing debate around political narratives in films, as he responded to claims that facts and timelines in Dhurandhar have been distorted to suit a particular ideology. He made it clear that propaganda in cinema is nothing new and has existed for decades.

title: Taylor Swift bodyguard mystery: Drew avoids her when Kelce is around
content: Taylor Swift’s long-standing bodyguard, Drew, reportedly adheres to strict rules. It includes never leaving her sight and maintaining a low public profile. This decade-long tenure, unlike her previous bodyguard Greg Dent, highlights a deep trust with Drew. He reportedly earns a significant salary. His dedication is noteworthy especially after a past sexual assault incident where her previous bodyguard reportedly did not intervene.

title: ‘We’ll see him...’: MI coach Jayawardene drops big Rohit Sharma fitness update
content: Mumbai Indians' head coach Mahela Jayawardene offered a cautious update on Rohit Sharma's fitness ahead of their clash with Chennai Super Kings. Sharma was seen batting in the nets after a hamstring injury, but his return depends on the medical team's daily assessment. Jayawardene also highlighted the team's struggles with consistency and player availability, having used 20 players this season.

title: CPI slams ₹993 hike in commercial LPG prices
content: P. Sandosh Kumar said that instead of ensuring stability in fuel supplies and prices, this approach had left the country exposed

title: Govt. absorbed global oil shock, didn’t pass it on to consumers: Hardeep Puri
content: Petroleum Minister says India has remained resilient even after 60 days of supply disruption and attributed the stability to long-term planning and timely policy interventions by the government

title: Pact on foreign language learning set to provide global platform to Rajasthan’s youth
content: The MoU for a foreign language communication skills programme was signed between the State government and the English and Foreign Languages ​​University, Hyderabad, in the presence of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan

title: After Himanta’s remarks, Indian officials say commitment to normalise ties with Bangladesh unaffected
content: Stakeholders at multiple levels may look at India’s neighbourhood from their point of view; however, that cannot be interpreted as a reflection of Delhi’s current position, The Hindu was told

title: Assembly elections 2026 LIVE: Repolling begins at 15 booths in South 24 Parganas after ECI's repoll order
content: BJP strategy to defame Bengal: TMC's Shashi Panja alleges poll-day provocation after ECI orders repoll

title: Voting begins at 15 booths of two Assembly seats after ECI orders repoll in West Bengal
content: The repolls were ordered in 11 polling stations of Magrahat Paschim Assembly constituency and four in Diamond Harbour. Voting began at 7 a.m. and will continue till 6 p.m., the official say

title: India to tap augmented solar capacity, coal to weather El Nino, summer-related power demand
content: When India attained its peak demand for power on April 25, solar plants accounted for 21.5% of the power generated while coal plants stood at 66.9%

title: Nationwide cell broadcast test alert on May 2; people may receive emergency alert on mobile phones
content: During the exercise, citizens may receive an emergency alert notification on their mobile phones, which could be accompanied by a loud sound or vibration

title: Bihar CM Samrat Choudhary proposes system where the children of Ministers and the poor study together
content: He emphasised that, to ensure girls receive an education and remain safe, police personnel will be deployed and remain vigilant in the vicinity of all schools and colleges in Bihar.

title: Govt grants relief to firms hit by West Asia disruptions, extends contract deadlines without penalty
content: The relief applies to contracts with government agencies across goods, services and works, an Office Memorandum, issued by the Department of Expenditure, indicated, covering suppliers, consultants and contractors engaged in public procurement

title: Deletion under SIR, not EVMs pose serious threat to democracy: Omar Abdullah
content: Meanwhile, National Conference president Dr. Farooq Abdullah accused the Opposition Peoples Democratic Party of “not supporting his party in the Rajya Sabha elections”

title: First talks under new Manipur government held with Kuki-Zo insurgent groups
content: A joint statement by the KNO and the UPF said that the day-long discussions focused on operational and security-related issues and “both sides broadly agreed on the need to relocate certain SoO camps to reduce friction with local populations and improve security issues”

title: U.P. CM announces wage reform, warns industrial unrest will harm progress
content: Efforts are under way to also provide free annual health insurance coverage of ₹5 lakh to workers in industrial regions, says Adityanath; reaffirms commitment to ensuring stability in workers’ lives

title: Census 2027: Around 82 lakh households have opted for self enumeration as field visits begin in 5 states
content: The exercise will continue till May 30, 2026, during which trained enumerators will go house-to-house for data collection

title: Bengaluru hotels to hike prices again after commercial gas cylinder price goes up from ₹2,162 to ₹3,152
content: The price of a commercial LPG cylinder has seen an unprecedented hike of over 70% in just 60 days in Bengaluru, with three hikes and a withdrawal of ₹150 discount during this period

title: Sixth Nilgiri-class frigate joins fleet, boosting naval firepower
content: Mahendragiri is equipped with advanced sensors, and a comprehensive weapons suite capable of anti-surface, anti-air, and anti-submarine warfare operations

title: More flight cuts loom at Air India amid cost pressures
content: While airline sources said domestic flights have not been cut, the CEO indicated they are also under profitability pressure

title: Lt. Gen. Balbir Singh takes over as GOC 15 Corps in Srinagar
content: According to the Army, the Chinar Corps Commander reaffirmed his commitment to work in close synergy with the civil administration and the people to advance lasting stability and inclusive growth in the region

title: Scorching heat boosts salt production in Thoothukudi; glut concerns grow
content: Recalling last year’s season that was impacted by unseasonal rainfall, Association secretary adds that this year’s weather has been favourable, but anticipated stockpiling of salt has emerged as a major concern for the producers

title: AERB issues permission for major equipment erection at Kudankulam Nuclear Power Project
content: The KKNPP units incorporate many advanced safety features as per the requirements specified by AERB in its Safety Code on Design of Light Water Reactor-based NPPs

title: Independent media outlets and online critics: Far from a safe harbour
content: An infrastructure of censorship is emerging, enabling the Central and various State governments to take social media content down using existing laws and rules. Independent media outlets and online critics now find their reach at a precipice. Aroon Deep reports on how in an environment of misinformation and deepfakes, opposing voices also become targets

title: Tamil Nadu to experience hotter May, with above-normal rainfall
content: In its outlook, the IMD has also outlined the likely impact of increased temperature, including on agriculture, and issued an agromet advisory

title: A.P. govt. will set up ‘Labour Addas’ to upskill workers, says CM Naidu
content: 15 Labour Addas to be set up in the first phase; CM distributes ₹5 lakh each to 35 construction workers  and presents Shrama Shakti awards to workers and industrial managements, marking the May Day

title: ‘Sustainable healthcare practices key to future’
content: Doctors’ role critical in helping the country achieve its vision of becoming a developed nation by 2047, says the director of the National Institute for Research in Environmental Health at the graduation day of Kodagu Institute of Medical Sciences in Madikeri

title: India’s first State-led Centre of Excellence for space tech launched in Bengaluru
content: The idea is to drive advanced research and innovation in space technologies, industry-aligned training and talent development, startup incubation and ecosystem building and cross-sector applications of

title: Senior official penalised for delay in providing information under RTI Act
content: Petitioner said the holding back of information led to her mother’s property being sold to a third party

title: ₹1 lakh crore investment push for aerospace, defence section in Andhra Pradesh
content: The DRDO defence manufacturing facility, for which 350 acres has been allocated in Puttaparthi,  is poised to be one of country’s largest aerospace and defence research hubs

title: Leadership change: High command’s decision is prasada for us, says D.K. Shivakumar
content: Karnataka Home Minister G. Parameshwara has said that Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge’s statement puts a full stop to speculation about leadership change

title: Three of a family from Tiruchi among Madhya Pradesh boat tragedy victims
content: Kamaraj, wife Karkuzhali, and young son Tamilventhan died in Jabalpur accident; elder son survives; family awaits bodies

title: Piduguralla sizzles at 45.9°C; 64 mandals in A.P. likely to witness heatwave on May 2
content: Light rains accompanied by thunderstorms and lightning are likely at isolated places in Srikakulam, Vizianagaram, ASR, Polavaram, Visakhapatnam, Chittoor and Tirupati districts, says APSDMA

title: Constant churn in Karnataka CMO in this tenure of Siddaramaiah raises eyebrows
content: The present tenure of Siddaramaiah has seen four intelligence chiefs so far and three ACSs. Two of his political secretaries have been sacked

title: Bedroll attendant arrested for ‘raping’ woman on train
content: The accused allegedly raped the woman passenger on board Visakha Express on April 26, says GRP official

title: Bengal Assembly Elections 2026 Voting Live Updates: Repolling Underway At 15 Booths
content: Bengal Assembly Elections 2026 Voting Live Updates: The repolls were ordered in 11 polling stations of the Magrahat Paschim assembly constituency and four in Diamond Harbour.

title: Trump's "Lunatics" Jab At Iran Over Nukes As US Clock To End War Stops
content: Rejecting Iran's latest proposal to end the war presented on Friday, Trump said the conflict would not end prematurely.

title: 2 Days Before Counting, Bengal Holds Repolling At 15 Booths After Complaints
content: Bengal Assembly Elections 2026: Repolling At 15 Booths In Bengal's Diamond Harbour, Magrahat Paschim Begins After Complaints Of Irregularities: Trinamool, Mamata Banerjee, BJP Suvendu Adhikari

title: Centre Gives Contractors Extension, Waives Penalties Amid Middle East War
content: The relief applies to contracts with government agencies across goods, services and works, covering suppliers, consultants and contractors engaged in public procurement.

title: Why 60-day War Powers Resolution Deadline Doesn't Constrain Presidents
content: The Trump administration signaled on April 30, 2026, that it would ignore that deadline, set by the War Powers Resolution.

title: Middle East, Europe Would Have Blown To Pieces: Trump On Iran Nuclear Threat
content: US President Donald Trump on Friday said military action was taken against Iran's nuclear programme to save the Gulf region, including Israel, from the threat posed if Tehran had been successful in having a nuclear weapon.

title: "Blast Or Make Deal": Trump Outlines Iran War Options
content: US President Donald Trump on Friday said the United States faces a stark choice in its approach to Iran, either to pursue a negotiated settlement or escalate militarily.

title: 5 Killed After Small Plane Crashes In US' Texas
content: A small plane carrying pickleball players crashed among trees in Texas Hill Country, killing all five people aboard, authorities said Friday.

title: US Withdrawing 5,000 Troops From Germany
content: The United States is withdrawing 5,000 troops from Germany, the Pentagon announced on Friday, in an apparent US rebuke to the close NATO ally amid a widening rift between President Donald Trump and Europe over the Iran war.

title: Trump Tells US Congress Hostilities In Iran "Have Terminated"
content: President Donald Trump told top US lawmakers on Friday that hostilities in Iran had ended, after coming under pressure from Congress to seek authorization for the conflict as it headed into its third month.

title: UP Groom Shot Dead On Way To Wedding: Cops
content: A 27-year-old groom on the way to his wedding was shot dead by unidentified assailants here on Friday evening, triggered panic in the area, police said.

title: Two Men Drown While Bathing In Canal In UP: Cops
content: Two men drowned while bathing in a canal on Friday after they apparently ventured into deep waters and were caught in the strong current, police said.

title: US Catholic Archdiocese To Pay $800 Million To Settle 1,300 Sex Abuse Cases
content: In addition to the $800 million payment, the Archdiocese agreed to release documents pertaining to sexual offenders in the Church.

title: Crude, Refining, Distribution: How Retail Price Of Gas Is Calculated
content: Because the price of crude oil is the largest element, most of the price at the pump is derived from the global oil market.

title: Pope Leo Names Former Undocumented Migrant As US Bishop
content: The Vatican in a statement announced the nomination of 56-year-old Evelio Menjivar-Ayala, currently an auxiliary bishop in Washington.

title: Enforcement Directorate Awards 2 Officers Who Were Part Of I-PAC Raids
content: Assistant Directors Vikram Ahlawat and Prashant Chandila received their commendation certificates from Minister of State for Finance Pankaj Chaudhary.

title: "Why Would I Tell You That?" Trump Evades Reporter's Question On Iran Strikes
content: Trump said that negotiations were ongoing but inconsistent. "They get close, and then a new group of people comes in," he said, pointing to shifting positions within Iran's leadership.

title: US Military-Run Mission For Gaza Aid Set To Close Soon: Report
content: The closing of the Civil-Military Coordination Centre (CMCC) in Israel would mark the latest blow to President Donald Trump's Gaza plan

title: Ladakh sets in motion process for preparing first ever 'Master Plan' for UT
content: a 'Master plan' has been signed between the UT and school of planning and architecture (SPA) in ladakh . the first draft is likely to be submitted by SPA within three months . SPA will prepare a comprehensive plan for aldakh ecosystems .
